2604.21416.title: CSC: Turning the Adversary's Poison against Itself
2604.21416.paper_summary: Poisoning-based backdoor attacks pose significant threats to deep neural networks by embedding triggers in training data, causing models to misclassify triggered inputs as adversary-specified labels while maintaining performance on clean data. Existing poison restraint-based defenses often suffer from inadequate detection against specific attack variants and compromise model utility through unlearning methods that lead to accuracy degradation. This paper conducts a comprehensive analysis of backdoor attack dynamics during model training, revealing that poisoned samples form isolated clusters in latent space early on, with triggers acting as dominant features distinct from benign ones. Leveraging these insights, we propose Cluster Segregation Concealment (CSC), a novel poison suppression defense. CSC first trains a deep neural network via standard supervised learning while segregating poisoned samples through feature extraction from early epochs, DBSCAN clustering, and identification of anomalous clusters based on class diversity and density metrics. In the concealment stage, identified poisoned samples are relabeled to a virtual class, and the model's classifier is fine-tuned using cross-entropy loss to replace the backdoor association with a benign virtual linkage, preserving overall accuracy. CSC was evaluated on four benchmark datasets against twelve poisoning-based attacks, CSC outperforms nine state-of-the-art defenses by reducing average attack success rates to near zero with minimal clean accuracy loss. Contributions include robust backdoor patterns identification, an effective concealment mechanism, and superior empirical validation, advancing trustworthy artificial intelligence.
2604.21416.paper_summary_zh: 基於毒害的後門攻擊對深度神經網絡構成了重大威脅，通過在訓練數據中嵌入觸發器，導致模型將觸發的輸入錯誤分類為對手指定的標籤，同時在乾淨數據上保持性能。現有的毒害約束防禦通常在針對特定攻擊變種的檢測上不足，並通過不學習的方法妥協模型效用，導致準確性下降。本文對模型訓練過程中的後門攻擊動態進行了全面分析，揭示了毒害樣本在潛在空間中早期形成孤立的聚類，觸發器作為與良性特徵不同的主導特徵。利用這些見解，我們提出了集群隔離隱蔽（CSC），這是一種新穎的毒害抑制防禦。CSC首先通過標準監督學習訓練深度神經網絡，同時通過從早期時期的特徵提取、DBSCAN聚類以及根據類別多樣性和密度指標識別異常聚類來隔離毒害樣本。在隱蔽階段，識別出的毒害樣本被重新標記為虛擬類別，並使用交叉熵損失對模型的分類器進行微調，以用良性的虛擬連結替換後門關聯，從而保持整體準確性。CSC在四個基準數據集上對十二種基於毒害的攻擊進行了評估，CSC的表現超過了九種最先進的防禦，將平均攻擊成功率降低到接近零，同時最小化乾淨準確性的損失。貢獻包括穩健的後門模式識別、有效的隱蔽機制和優越的實證驗證，推進了可信的人工智慧。
2604.21416.author: Yuchen Shi et.al.
2604.21416.authors: Yuchen Shi, Xin Guo, Huajie Chen, Tianqing Zhu, Bo Liu, Wanlei Zhou
